text:
schema A: create-entry, description "Creates an entry", one param: data

schema B: create_customer, clear description, typed params with descriptions

same LLM, same task, opposite results. A gets garbage. B gets the right call.

this is why we grade MCP schemas. full writeup:

the cheap schema is more expensive.

F-grade MCP servers: avg 231 tokens/tool + model confusion + retries.
A+-grade servers: avg 45 tokens/tool + correct first try.

that 5x token cost is before you count the failed attempts.

198 servers graded: https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
